---
title: "Surgery Group 2"
name: "Ale, Daniella, Aaliyah"
date: "2026-02-27"
output:
  html_document:1
    theme: spacelab
    toc: no

---

## Load the libraries
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(shiny)
library(shinythemes)
library(janitor)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


```{r}
surgery <- read_csv("data/surgery.csv") %>% 
  clean_names()
```
1. What are the variable names?
```{r}
names(surgery)
```
2.What is the distribution of patient age and race?
```{r}
surgery %>% 
  filter(race!="NA") %>% 
  group_by(race) %>% 
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    min_age=min(age, na.rm = T),
    max_age=max(age, na.rm = T),
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
    median_age = median(age, na.rm = T)
  ) %>% 
  arrange(desc(n))
```

3.Is age associated with mortality? 
```{r}
surgery %>% 
  group_by(mort30) %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
    sd_age = sd(age, na.rm = T),
    n = n()
  )
```

4. Is the relationship between age and mortality consistent across different months?
```{r}
surgery %>% 
  group_by(month, mort30) %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
    n = n()
  )
```

5. How many males and females were sampled in this study?
```{r}
surgery %>% 
  filter(gender!="NA") %>% 
  count(gender)
```

```{r}
 surgery %>% 
  filter(gender != "NA") %>% 
  count(gender) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = gender, y = n, fill = gender)) +
  geom_col(fill= "turquoise4") +
  labs(title = "Number of Males and Females",
       x = "Gender",
       y = "Count")
```

6.Is mortality higher in males or females?
```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(gender != "NA") %>%
  count(gender, mort30)
```

```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(gender != "NA") %>%
  group_by(gender) %>%
  summarize(mortality = sum(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = gender, y = mortality)) +
  geom_col(fill = "turquoise4") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by Gender",
       x = "Gender",
       y = "Number of Deaths")
```

7.What month is mortality highest in?
```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(month != "NA") %>%
   mutate(month = factor(month, c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"))) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarize(mortality = sum(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T)) %>%
  arrange(month)
```

```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(month != "NA") %>%
  mutate(month = factor(month, c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"))) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarize(mortality = sum(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = mortality)) +
  geom_col(fill = "turquoise4") +
  labs(title = "Deaths by Month",
       x = "Month",
       y = "Number of Deaths")
```

8. What complication shows the highest mortality?
```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(mort30 == "Yes") %>%
  select(baseline_cancer, baseline_cvd, baseline_dementia,
         baseline_diabetes, baseline_digestive,
         baseline_osteoart, baseline_psych,
         baseline_pulmonary) %>%
  summarise_all(~sum(. == "Yes", na.rm = T))
```
```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(mort30 == "Yes") %>%
  select(baseline_cancer, baseline_cvd, baseline_dementia,
         baseline_diabetes, baseline_digestive,
         baseline_osteoart, baseline_psych,
         baseline_pulmonary) %>%
  summarise_all(~sum(. == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "complication",
               values_to = "deaths") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(complication, deaths), y = deaths)) +
  geom_col(fill= "turquoise4") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Deaths by Baseline Complication",
       x = "Complication",
       y = "Number of Deaths")
```

9.Does ASA status predict mortality?
```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(asa_status!="NA") %>% 
  group_by(asa_status) %>%
  summarise(mortality_rate = mean(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T))
```

```{r}
surgery %>%
  filter(asa_status != "NA") %>% 
  group_by(asa_status) %>%
  summarize(mortality_rate = mean(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = asa_status, y = mortality_rate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "turquoise4") +
  labs(title = "Mortality Rate by ASA Status",
       x = "ASA Status",
       y = "Mortality Rate")
```

10.Does time of day affect outcomes differently for high-risk patients?

```{r}
surgery <- surgery %>% 
  mutate(high_risk = asa_status %in% c("III", "IV", "V"),
         hour_group = floor(hour)) 
```

```{r}
surgery %>% 
  group_by(high_risk, hour_group) %>% 
  summarize(
    mortality_rate = mean(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = hour_group,
             y = mortality_rate,
             color = high_risk))+
  geom_line()+
  theme_minimal()
```

11.Is there any association between moon phase and mortality?
```{r}
moon <- surgery %>% 
  group_by(moonphase) %>% 
  summarize(
    mortality_rate = mean(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
moon %>% 
   ggplot(aes(x = moonphase,
             y = mortality_rate,
             fill = moonphase))+
  geom_col()+
  theme_minimal()
```
19			
Feb	17			
Mar	12			
Apr	12			
May	10			
Jun	14			
Jul	12			
Aug	9			
Sep	16			
Oct	8	
Nov	5			
Dec	4	
```{r}
death_by_month <- surgery %>%
  filter(month != "NA") %>%
   mutate(month = factor(month, c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"))) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarize(mortality = sum(mort30 == "Yes", na.rm = T)) %>%
  arrange(month)
```

12. Create a Shiny App...
the range of death numbers filtered by month
```{r, eval=F, echo=T}
ui <- dashboardPage(
  dashboardHeader(title = "The Range of Number of Deaths of Patients by Month"),
  dashboardSidebar(
    selectInput(
      "x"= "Select Month",
      choices= unique(death_by_month$month),
      multiple = TRUE,
        selected = unique(death_by_month$month)
    ),
    dashboardBody(
      plotOutput("plot", width="600px", height= "500px")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$plot <- renderPlot({
    
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```
